13
11
17

However, as you can see in the project attachments, 
there are still problems.

by literally disambiguiating it 
    SecurityEventType.ForcedPasswordChangeCompleted);
to
    MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication.SecurityEventType.ForcedPasswordChangeCompleted);

Done
Everything verified. Copying the five full files to outputs with their repo directory structure preserved:


Ran a command, read a file
Ran a command, read a file

Copying the five fixed full files to outputs preserving paths

Presented 5 files
Done
Five full files above, each replacing the file at the matching path under /home/kushal/src/dotnet/myrestaurant/. Before writing them, I reconstructed all 123 files from dump.txt (SHA-256 verified byte-exact), installed PostgreSQL 16, loaded your 0001_initial_schema.sql, and reproduced the exact 42702 error — then confirmed every fixed query runs clean.

The test failure — src/MyRestaurant.DataAccess/Identity/DapperUserStore.cs

The PersonColumns constant listed bare column names. That's fine for the three single-table lookups, but GetUsersInRoleAsync joins person_role, which also has a person_identifier column — so the first name in the SELECT list is ambiguous (hence POSITION: 8, right after SELECT ). The fix qualifies every column in the constant with person., which resolves the JOIN and is harmless everywhere else. I ran all four interpolated queries (FindById, FindByName, FindByEmail, GetUsersInRole with a seeded kitchen grant and case-insensitive KITCHEN input) against your real schema — all pass, and the JOIN returns the expected row. This is the only change in that file.

The four BL0008 warnings — the razor pages

I checked the actual analyzer source in dotnet/aspnetcore rather than guessing: SupplyParameterFromFormAnalyzer.IsDefaultValueInitializer explicitly ignores null, null!, default, and default! — only "real" initializers like = new() trip it. So each page now uses the exact pattern the official .NET 10 Blazor template uses (verified in LoginWith2fa.razor upstream): Input { get; set; } = default!; plus Input ??= new(); at the top of OnInitialized(Async). This keeps the property non-nullable, so no CS8602 fallout in the markup. SignIn.razor had no lifecycle method, so it gains a one-line OnInitialized; the other three get the ??= line at the top of their existing OnInitializedAsync.

One small tidy-up in ChangePasswordRequired.razor: your fully-qualified MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication.SecurityEventType... fix was correct — the root cause is that _Imports.razor never imports that namespace. I added a file-local @using MyRestaurant.Domain.Authentication and shortened the call back to SecurityEventType.ForcedPasswordChangeCompleted. I checked the namespace's types (ObligationsPipeline, SecurityEventType, SignInAudit, two enums) for collisions with anything in scope — there are none. If you prefer your FQN version, it's equally valid; this is just the idiomatic spelling of the same fix.

One honest caveat: no .NET 10 SDK is reachable from my sandbox, so I couldn't run dotnet build end-to-end — but the SQL is proven against Postgres 16 with your schema, and the razor changes are verbatim the upstream template pattern with anchored, minimal edits. Expect a clean build, 0 warnings, and 242 passing.


Dapperuserstore
CS 

Changepasswordrequired
RAZOR 

Signin
RAZOR 

Signinrecoverycode
RAZOR 

Signintwofactor
RAZOR 

Want to be notified when Claude responds?






Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.




42
14
23
